terms.definition:
A language-neutral contract for information systems that must remain usable
under persistent disagreement.
terms.label: Accountable Record
terms.plain_language:
A structured record system for keeping information inspectable and usable even
when people disagree about meaning, authority, correctness, or interpretation.

terms.definition:
An incremental level of Accountable Record adoption that describes which
record structure, identifiers, elements, checks, and exports a system supports.
terms.label: Adoption Level
terms.plain_language: A step in how fully a system has adopted Accountable Records.

terms.definition:
A short registry-scoped name used in compact identifiers to refer to a
namespace authority.
terms.label: Authority Alias
terms.plain_language: A short name for the organization or authority that owns a namespace.

terms.definition:
AR preserves the structure needed for disagreement to remain inspectable. AR
does not resolve disagreement.
terms.label: Boundary Statement

terms.definition:
A machine-readable artifact exported by a system for inspection, validation,
exchange, or review.
terms.label: Bundle
terms.plain_language: The exported package a system provides so others can inspect the record.

terms.definition:
A durable URI used as the preferred public identifier for an Accountable
Record artifact, element type, element package, schema, or related resource.
terms.label: Canonical URI
terms.plain_language: The durable web-style identifier for something in Accountable Records.

terms.definition:
The Accountable Record contract that defines additive, compatible, breaking,
deprecated, superseded, and withdrawn changes for elements, packages, schemas,
exports, and conformance semantics.
terms.label: Change Contract
terms.plain_language: The rules for what can change without breaking systems that depend on AR.

terms.definition: An assertion preserved within an Accountable Record.
terms.label: Claim
terms.plain_language: Something the record says and keeps available for checking.

terms.definition: The result produced when an applicable claim is checked against a bundle.
terms.label: Claim Outcome
terms.plain_language: The result of checking a claim.

terms.definition: A declared structural form describing how a claim is checked by a verifier.
terms.label: Claim Shape
terms.plain_language: The kind of check a claim uses.

terms.definition:
A check, claim, trait, mapping, transformation rule, conformance rule, or
profile rule used to detect a structural collapse.
terms.label: Collapse Detection
terms.plain_language: A way to find a record collapse.

terms.definition:
A recurrent form of structural collapse in which a record fuses things that AR
requires to remain separable.
terms.label: Collapse Pattern
terms.plain_language: A recurring pattern where a record mixes things that should stay separate.

terms.definition:
Guidance for repairing, avoiding, or restructuring records that exhibit a
structural collapse.
terms.label: Collapse Remediation
terms.plain_language: Guidance for fixing or avoiding a record collapse.

terms.definition:
A human-readable and tool-friendly identifier composed from an authority alias,
project, component group, and local element or package name.
terms.label: Compact ID
terms.plain_language: A shorter identifier that tools and people can use instead of the full URI.

terms.definition:
A canonical example, bundle, or expected report used to check whether
independent implementations preserve Accountable Record semantics.
terms.label: Compatibility Fixture
terms.plain_language: A shared test example used to make sure different tools behave the same way.

terms.definition:
A stable architectural category that organizes related verifiable element
types.
terms.label: Component Group
terms.plain_language: A named group that organizes related kinds of verifiable elements.

terms.definition:
A claim shape or verification behavior supplied by a domain, local,
proprietary, or profile-specific implementation when declarative Accountable
Record claim shapes are insufficient.
terms.label: Custom Check
terms.plain_language:
A check supplied by a specific domain or tool because the standard shapes are
not enough.

terms.definition:
A domain-specific profile that defines domain record types, domain claims,
domain traits, field mappings, transformation kinds, and profile-specific
requirements.
terms.label: Domain Profile
terms.plain_language: A domain-specific way of using Accountable Records.

terms.definition: A concrete occurrence of an element type within an Accountable Record.
terms.label: Element Instance
terms.plain_language: One actual verifiable element inside a record.

terms.definition:
A reusable distribution unit that packages one or more verifiable element
types with their schemas, checks, examples, mappings, verifier expectations,
compatibility fixtures, expected reports, dependencies, and rationale.
terms.label: Element Package
terms.plain_language:
A reusable package of related verifiable elements and the files needed to
check and use them.

terms.definition:
A declared requirement that an element package depends on another element
package, element type, contract feature, schema, mapping, or compatibility
fixture.
terms.label: Element Package Dependency
terms.plain_language: Something an element package needs in order to work correctly.

terms.definition:
A canonical example input, bundle, element instance, profile, report, or test
case distributed with an element package to demonstrate and test its expected
behavior.
terms.label: Element Package Fixture
terms.plain_language: A test example included with an element package.

terms.definition:
A declared kind of verifiable element that defines structure, checks,
examples, exports, and compatibility expectations.
terms.label: Element Type
terms.plain_language: A reusable kind of verifiable element.

terms.definition:
A legacy or upstream-facing category used to describe the kind of accountable
entity or record subject a record concerns.
terms.label: Entity Kind
terms.plain_language: An older or external term for the kind of thing a record is about.

terms.definition: Structured support used in verification, review, or conformance checking.
terms.label: Evidence
terms.plain_language: Material used to support or check a claim.

terms.definition:
A canonical report expected from running a specified verifier, profile, claim
set, or element package against a specified fixture.
terms.label: Expected Report
terms.plain_language: The report a verifier should produce for a given test example.

terms.definition:
A recurrent structural failure pattern that makes records harder to inspect,
verify, transform, compare, or preserve under disagreement.
terms.label: Failure Mode
terms.plain_language: A common way records become unreliable or hard to inspect.

terms.definition:
A declared mapping between a field, path, property, column, key, or local data
shape in a source system and the structural role expected by an Accountable
Record element type, package, profile, or verifier.
terms.label: Field Mapping
terms.plain_language: A rule that says which local field plays which Accountable Record role.

terms.definition:
A named structural role that a field or value may play within an Accountable
Record, element type, element package, mapping guide, profile, or verifier
run.
terms.label: Field Role
terms.plain_language: The job a field performs in an Accountable Record.

terms.definition:
A stable name, key, URI, compact ID, persistent ID, or instance ID used to
refer to an Accountable Record artifact or verifiable element.
terms.label: Identifier
terms.plain_language: A stable name used to refer to something in an Accountable Record.

terms.definition:
The Accountable Record contract that defines how authorities, project spaces,
element types, element instances, packages, schemas, exported artifacts, and
lock entries are identified.
terms.label: Identity Contract
terms.plain_language: The rules for naming things so they stay stable and do not collide.

terms.definition:
A claim that must be run and satisfied for a declared maturity level,
conformance profile, element package, or verifier run.
terms.label: Mandatory Claim
terms.plain_language: A claim that must be checked and satisfied.

terms.definition:
An incremental Accountable Record adoption level that specifies which
requirements, checks, structures, and verification expectations apply at a
given stage of conformance.
terms.label: Maturity Level
terms.plain_language: A step in how fully a system has adopted Accountable Records.

terms.definition:
An organization, domain, registry, or owner responsible for assigning stable
names within a project space.
terms.label: Namespace Authority
terms.plain_language: The organization or authority responsible for a set of names.

terms.definition:
A transformation behavior used when an operation does not raise an identity
question for the represented subject.
terms.label: No Identity Question
terms.plain_language: A change-like operation that does not affect the identity of the subject.

terms.definition:
A claim that may be run when selected by a bundle, profile, element package,
or verifier configuration but does not block an achieved maturity level unless
a profile explicitly makes it mandatory.
terms.label: Optional Claim
terms.plain_language:
A claim that can be checked but does not block the level unless a profile says
it must.

terms.definition:
The Accountable Record contract that defines what an element package
guarantees to downstream systems and how packages are composed, versioned,
resolved, locked, and trusted across project boundaries.
terms.label: Package Contract
terms.plain_language:
The rules for reusable AR packages and how other systems can safely depend on
them.

terms.definition:
An optional non-HTTP durable identifier used for archival, private,
proprietary, internal, or non-web Accountable Record contexts.
terms.label: Persistent ID
terms.plain_language: A durable identifier that does not have to be a public web address.

terms.definition:
A declared set of claims, traits, mappings, requirements, or checks used to
describe or evaluate a class of Accountable Record artifacts.
terms.label: Profile
terms.plain_language: A declared way to check or describe a type of accountable record.

terms.definition:
A domain-specific failure mode defined by a profile and composed with AR core
failure modes.
terms.label: Profile Failure Mode
terms.plain_language: A failure mode specific to a domain profile.

terms.definition:
The process by which a profile declares the element packages, element types,
claims, traits, checks, mappings, requirements, or adoption levels that apply
to a verifier run or exported bundle.
terms.label: Profile Selection
terms.plain_language: The profile's declaration of what should be loaded and checked.

terms.definition:
A stable project, package, repository, product, standard, or domain space
within a namespace authority.
terms.label: Project Space
terms.plain_language: The project area where a namespace authority defines related identifiers.

terms.definition:
The thing, event, decision, action, condition, artifact, or referent that an
Accountable Record is about.
terms.label: Record Subject
terms.plain_language: The thing the record is about.

terms.definition:
A record design failure in which two or more distinct structures that AR
requires to remain separable are fused into one field, assertion, identifier,
status, claim, or record.
terms.label: Structural Collapse
terms.plain_language:
When a record mixes separate things together so they can no longer be checked
cleanly.

terms.definition:
A classification assigned to a record subject by a profile, domain vocabulary,
external vocabulary, mapping guide, or verifier.
terms.label: Subject Classification
terms.plain_language: A category used to describe the subject of a record.

terms.definition:
A declared kind or category for the subject of a record, owned by a profile,
domain, authority, external vocabulary, or mapping guide rather than by AR as
a universal ontology.
terms.label: Subject Kind
terms.plain_language: The kind of thing the record is about.

terms.definition:
A declaration that a record, record type, profile, or package uses a specified
subject kind under a specified vocabulary or mapping context.
terms.label: Subject Kind Declaration
terms.plain_language: A statement declaring which subject kind is being used.

terms.definition:
A machine-readable mapping between a profile, domain, institutional, or
external subject vocabulary and AR subject structure.
terms.label: Subject Kind Mapping
terms.plain_language: A mapping from someone else's subject vocabulary into AR subject structure.

terms.definition:
A machine-readable and human-readable guide that helps a profile map local,
domain, institutional, or external subject vocabulary into AR subject
structure.
terms.label: Subject Mapping Guide
terms.plain_language: A guide for connecting existing subject terms to AR subject structure.

terms.definition:
A declaration that binds a profile-defined record type to the subject
structure expected for records of that type.
terms.label: Record Type Subject Binding
terms.plain_language: A rule saying what kind of subject a record type is about.

terms.definition:
A verifier-emitted artifact that records claim outcomes, evidence, and
structured verification results for a bundle.
terms.label: Report
terms.plain_language: The result produced after checking a bundle.

terms.definition:
A declarative commitment or structural guarantee made by a record type or
verifiable element type.
terms.label: Trait
terms.plain_language: A declared property or guarantee that a record type says it has.

terms.definition:
A declared relationship between records describing a change, correction,
successor relation, reclassification, re-encoding, or other operation whose
effect on subject identity may be checked.
terms.label: Transformation
terms.plain_language: A declared change or change-like relationship between records.

terms.definition:
A rule determining whether a claimed transformation behavior is possible for
the declared subject structure, subject mapping, transformation kind, and
profile context.
terms.label: Transformation Admissibility
terms.plain_language: A rule saying whether a claimed transformation behavior is allowed in context.

terms.definition: The AR-defined identity behavior claimed by a declared transformation.
terms.label: Transformation Behavior
terms.plain_language: What a declared change does to subject identity.

terms.definition:
A bundle-level declaration connecting a source record, target record,
profile-defined transformation kind, AR-defined transformation behavior, and
optional evidence or lineage metadata.
terms.label: Transformation Declaration
terms.plain_language: A statement that one record is related to another by a declared change.

terms.definition:
Evidence used to support or verify a declared transformation behavior,
lineage relation, or profile-specific transformation kind.
terms.label: Transformation Evidence
terms.plain_language: Evidence used to check a declared transformation.

terms.definition:
A profile-defined label for the domain-specific kind of transformation being
declared.
terms.label: Transformation Kind
terms.plain_language: The domain-specific name for a declared transformation.

terms.definition:
A declared successor or accountability-lineage relation preserved by an
identity-inheriting transformation.
terms.label: Transformation Lineage
terms.plain_language: The lineage that connects a prior subject to a successor subject.

terms.definition:
A profile-defined rule constraining recognized transformation kinds, connected
record types, required evidence, admissible behaviors, or lineage requirements.
terms.label: Transformation Profile Rule
terms.plain_language: A profile rule for how transformations work in a domain.

terms.definition:
An independently identifiable part of an Accountable Record that can be
claimed about, sourced, related, verified, exported, and reviewed.
terms.label: Verifiable Element
terms.plain_language: A part of an Accountable Record that can be checked on its own.

terms.definition:
The process of checking claims, elements, bundles, profiles, or reports using
defined methods, evidence, checks, and outcomes.
terms.label: Verification
terms.plain_language: The act of checking something in an Accountable Record.

terms.definition:
A tool, system, process, or reviewer that loads a bundle, applies an
applicable profile or claim set, runs checks, and emits a report.
terms.label: Verifier
terms.plain_language: Something or someone that checks a bundle and produces a report.

terms.definition:
A declared hint, requirement, or compatibility expectation describing how a
verifier should load, interpret, check, or report on an element type or
element package.
terms.label: Verifier Expectation
terms.plain_language: A statement about what a verifier needs to do to use an element faithfully.
